question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record that mixes FRACTIONAL_FEE entries for a NON_FUNGIBLE_UNIQUE token, exploiting the absence of token-type validation in updateCustomFees() (the comment says 'Only FT can have fractional fee' but no enforcement exists), causing the mirror node to persist fractional fees on an NFT token in violation of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record that includes ROYALTY_FEE entries for a FUNGIBLE_COMMON token, exploiting the absence of token-type enforcement in updateCustomFees() (the comment says 'Only NFT can have royalty fee' but no guard exists), causing the mirror node to persist royalty fees on a fungible token in violation of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with duplicate collector account IDs across multiple fee entries, exploiting the absence of a uniqueness check in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to persist multiple fees with the same collector that misrepresent the actual fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record where the tokenId in the transaction body does not match the tokenId in the transaction entity, exploiting any mismatch between getEntity() and doUpdateTransaction() resolution, causing the mirror node to associate fee updates with the wrong token?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEmptyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record where all fee lists are initialized but empty (not null), exploiting the CollectionUtils.isEmpty() check in isEmptyFee() which returns true for empty lists, causing the mirror node to skip persisting the CustomFee row and silently drop the fee schedule update?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: addFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where addFixedFee() is called with a FixedFee object that has a null collectorAccountId, exploiting the absence of a null check on the fee's collectorAccountId before adding to the list, causing the mirror node to persist a fixed fee with a null collector that violates the fee schedule design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: addFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where addFractionalFee() is called with a FractionalFee that has denominator=0 and numerator=0, exploiting the absence of field validation before list insertion, causing the mirror node to persist a 0/0 fractional fee that represents an undefined fee rate?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: addRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where addRoyaltyFee() is called with a RoyaltyFee that has both numerator and denominator set to Long.MAX_VALUE, exploiting the absence of overflow validation, causing the mirror node to persist an extreme royalty fee fraction that misrepresents the NFT fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isChargedInToken()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FixedFee where denominatingTokenId equals the attached tokenId, exploiting the isChargedInToken() equality check in FixedFee, causing the mirror node to incorrectly trigger auto-association logic for a fee that should not be charged in the token itself?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isChargedInToken()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FractionalFee.isChargedInToken() always returns true regardless of context, causing the mirror node to always add the collector to autoAssociatedAccounts during token creation even when the fee configuration does not warrant auto-association?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setTotalSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a token transfer record with a negative totalSupply delta, exploiting the aggregation logic in AbstractToken.setTotalSupply() that adds negative values to the existing totalSupply, causing the mirror node to store a totalSupply that is inconsistent with the actual on-chain supply and misrepresents the token's economic parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: setTotalSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated negative totalSupply adjustments via crafted token dissociate records for deleted tokens, exploiting the negative-value aggregation path in setTotalSupply(), causing the mirror node to store a totalSupply of zero or negative that misrepresents the token's actual supply?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FractionalFee where the maximumAmount field is set to a value less than minimumAmount, exploiting the conditional check in parseFractionalFee() that only sets maximumAmount when it is nonzero (if maximumAmount != 0), causing the mirror node to store a fee schedule where the maximum constraint is silently dropped when set to zero?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a consensusTimestamp that is earlier than the existing fee schedule's timestamp, exploiting the Range.atLeast() timestamp assignment in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to create a fee history entry with an overlapping or incorrect timestamp range that corrupts historical fee lookups?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a FixedFee denominatingTokenId that self-references the token being updated, exploiting the EntityId.EMPTY substitution logic in parseFixedFee() where an empty denominatingTokenId is replaced with the attached tokenId, causing the mirror node to record a circular fee denomination that violates design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseRoyaltyFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RoyaltyFee for a FUNGIBLE_COMMON token where the fallback fee denominatingTokenId is set to the NFT token itself, exploiting the absence of token-type validation in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist a royalty fee with a self-referential fallback denomination that violates the design constraint that fees cannot be paid in NFTs?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a mix of valid and invalid fee entries (some with FEE_NOT_SET), exploiting the continue statement in the default branch of updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to partially persist only the valid fees while silently dropping the invalid ones, resulting in a fee schedule that differs from what was submitted?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record where the same fee collector account is used as both a fixed fee collector and a fractional fee collector, exploiting the absence of cross-fee-type collector uniqueness validation in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to persist a fee schedule with overlapping collector roles that misrepresents the actual fee structure?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFixedFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FixedFee with a denominatingTokenId pointing to a token of type NON_FUNGIBLE_UNIQUE, exploiting the absence of token-type validation in parseFixedFee() (fixed fees must be paid in fungible tokens or HBAR), causing the mirror node to persist a fixed fee denominated in an NFT that violates design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a FractionalFee where netOfTransfers is set to true for a token that does not support net-of-transfers semantics, exploiting the absence of a netOfTransfers compatibility check in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to store and expose a fee schedule with an incorrect netOfTransfers flag?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a RoyaltyFee that has both numerator=0 and denominator=0, exploiting the absence of zero-value validation in parseRoyaltyFee(), causing the mirror node to persist a 0/0 royalty fee fraction that represents an undefined exchange value fraction?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with a FixedFee where the collectorAccountId is the same as the token's treasuryAccountId, exploiting the absence of a treasury-as-collector restriction in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to persist a fee schedule where the treasury collects fees from itself?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record with an extremely large number of duplicate fee entries (same fee type, same collector, same amount), exploiting the absence of deduplication logic in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to persist redundant fee entries that inflate the stored fee schedule beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: parseFractionalFee()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FractionalFee with numerator=Long.MAX_VALUE and denominator=Long.MIN_VALUE, exploiting the absence of overflow/underflow checks in parseFractionalFee(), causing the mirror node to store a fee fraction that overflows when computed, misrepresenting the actual fee rate?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: updateCustomFees()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFEESCHEDULEUPDATE record where the tokenId in the transaction body references a token that has been deleted, exploiting the absence of a token-deletion check in updateCustomFees(), causing the mirror node to persist fee schedule updates for a deleted token that should no longer accept fee modifications?
